title: YouTube-style video platform — full feature polish

requirements.text: Redesign the HomePage to closely resemble YouTube's layout: a sticky top navigation bar with logo, search bar, and auth controls; a horizontal category filter chip row below the nav; a responsive video grid (1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, 3–4 on desktop) showing video thumbnails with title, channel name, view count, and relative upload time.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page grid renders at least 3 columns on desktop screens (≥1280px)
Each video card shows thumbnail, title, channel name, view count, and relative time
Category filter chips are horizontally scrollable on mobile
Top nav is sticky and contains logo, search, and auth controls

requirements.text: Implement a YouTube-style video watch page: the video player occupies the full left column on desktop; below the player show the video title, view count, upload date, action buttons (Like, Share, Save to Playlist), and the channel info row with avatar, subscriber count, and Subscribe button. The right column shows an 'Up next' recommended videos list.
acceptanceCriteria: Video player is displayed in a large left column (≥65% width on desktop)
Title, view count, and upload date are shown below the player
Channel avatar, name, subscriber count, and Subscribe button are present below the video info
Recommended videos list is shown in a right sidebar on desktop
Layout stacks vertically on mobile

requirements.text: Add a YouTube-style left sidebar navigation (collapsible on desktop, hidden on mobile replaced by bottom nav) with links to: Home, Shorts, Subscriptions, Playlists, Community, and Upload. Show icons alongside labels. Collapse to icon-only mode when toggled.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar is visible on desktop (≥1024px) with icons and labels
Sidebar can be collapsed to icon-only mode via a hamburger/menu toggle
All listed navigation links are present and functional
On mobile, sidebar is hidden and bottom navigation handles routing

requirements.text: Style the Shorts page as a vertical full-screen reel feed (TikTok/YouTube Shorts style): each short fills the viewport height, videos autoplay when scrolled into view and pause when out of view, with overlaid controls for Like, Comment, Share, and Subscribe.
acceptanceCriteria: Each short video occupies 100vh
Videos autoplay on scroll into view and pause when scrolled out
Like, Comment, Share, and Subscribe action buttons are overlaid on the video
Page is accessible via /shorts route

requirements.text: Apply a dark-first visual theme across the entire app inspired by YouTube's dark mode: near-black backgrounds (#0f0f0f for the main surface, #212121 for cards/sidebar), white primary text, muted gray secondary text, and red (#ff0000) as the primary accent color for subscribe buttons, active nav indicators, and the logo mark. Ensure consistent typography using a clean sans-serif font.
acceptanceCriteria: Background color is near-black (#0f0f0f or equivalent dark token)
Subscribe button and active nav indicators use red accent
Card backgrounds use a slightly lighter dark tone for visual separation
Text contrast meets readability standards (white on dark)

requirements.text: Add a Search Results page that displays results in the same video grid format as the home page, with a 'Search results for "<query>"' heading. Results should be fetched using the existing useSearchVideos hook and show loading skeletons while fetching.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /search?q=<term> shows results matching the query
A heading displays the search term
Loading skeletons appear while results are loading
Empty state message shown when no results found

requirements.text: Implement a Channel page accessible at /channel/:principalId that displays the channel banner, avatar, channel name, handle, subscriber count, and a Subscribe button. Below, show tabs for Videos, Shorts, Playlists, and Community, each rendering the relevant content using existing hooks (useGetChannelVideos, useGetCommunityPostsByChannel, useGetPlaylistById).
acceptanceCriteria: Channel page is accessible at /channel/:principalId
Channel banner, avatar, name, handle, subscriber count, and Subscribe button are displayed
Tabs for Videos, Shorts, Playlists, and Community are present and switch content
Videos tab shows the channel's uploaded videos in a grid

requirements.text: Ensure the backend supports all data required by the redesigned frontend: video metadata (title, description, thumbnail, duration, view count, upload timestamp, uploader principal), user profile (avatar, channel name, handle, subscriber count), comments, subscriptions, and playlists. Verify existing Motoko actor methods cover all these data shapes and add any missing query or update methods.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing query methods return complete video metadata including duration and thumbnail
User profile query returns avatar bytes, channel name, handle, and subscriber count
Subscription, comment, and playlist CRUD methods are all present
No frontend data fetch fails due to missing backend method

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor
Preserve all existing routes and functionality; only add or enhance
nonGoals: External CDN or video hosting integration
Third-party OAuth beyond Internet Identity and existing Google auth

required: YouTube-style red play button logo mark icon on dark background (mediatube-logo-icon.dim_128x128.png)
Mediatube full horizontal logo with red icon and white wordmark on transparent background (mediatube-logo-full.dim_320x80.png)
Generic channel banner gradient in dark red and charcoal tones (default-channel-banner.dim_1280x200.png)
